title: rubus silvaticus subsp. questieri
intro: Rubus silvaticus subsp. questieri, also known as Quester's woodland bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus silvaticus plant, a type of wild blackberry native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rubus silvaticus subsp. questieri include its ability to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help manage certain gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
